key_insight: A token-based dual-view framework improves breast cancer mammography classification by using dedicated fusion tokens to exchange information between CC and MLO views across multiple transformer depths.
clinical_relevance: Better integration of standard mammography views may help support more accurate BI-RADS classification and lesion assessment in screening and diagnostic workflows.
method: Freezes a vision transformer backbone and adds prompt-based adaptation for each view.
Uses dedicated fusion tokens to carry bidirectional CC↔MLO information via cross-attention.
Inserts fusion modules at multiple depths so shared information is refined progressively through the encoder.
evidence_signal: On VinDr-Mammo and CMMD, the method outperformed linear probing, prompt-only adaptation, and fusion baselines; on VinDr-Mammo BI-RADS classification it reached 50.40% F1-score and 0.8090 AUC.
watch_item: Results are benchmark-based and should be validated externally across diverse scanners, populations, and clinical settings before broad deployment.
paper_title: Token-Based Dual-view Fusion and Adaptation of Large Vision Models for Breast Cancer Classification
